data.title: Zoom's event highlights 'commitment to innovation,' Oppenheimer says
data.description:
• Zoom Communications (NASDAQ:ZM) made several announcements at yesterday's Zoomtopia event, with investment firm Oppenheimer saying the unveilings highlight Zoom's “commitment to innovation.” 
  • “AI Companion 3.0 was the key announcement from the event, which adds a number of new agentic skills to the product,” Oppenheimer analyst J. Parker Lane wrote in a note to clients. “The company's various growth drivers were unsurprisingly the focus of the event, as management noted that the team's top priority is accelerating the top-line. We believe Zoom continues to tell compelling stories around its AI monetization strategy and ZCX, and believe the investor session clearly displayed the company's confidence in realizing those visions.”
  • Lane kept his Hold rating on Zoom, but upped his price target to $90 from $80.
  • Aside from AI, Zoom's growth drivers are the same, Lane said: return its Online business unit to growth; continued progress in its Enterprise unit, including diversifying the product, continued progress in the core and bringing new routes to market. 
  • Shares rose 0.5% in early trading on Thursday.

data.title: AeroVironment rated Buy in new research coverage at Bank of America
data.description:
Bank of America has launched coverage of AeroVironment (NASDAQ:AVAV) with a Buy rating, citing strong growth prospects in military drone and counter-drone technologies.

The analysts highlighted AeroVironment’s (NASDAQ:AVAV) expanding portfolio, which includes autonomous systems, precision strike platforms, counter-UAS technologies, space-based systems, directed energy weapons and cyber-electronic warfare capabilities. They see the company as a key beneficiary of the U.S. Department of War’s (DoW, formerly DoD) heightened focus on low-cost “attritable” drones that can be deployed in swarms and customized with modular payloads.

Policy tailwinds and modular advantage

The bank pointed to Secretary of War Pete Hegseth’s “Unleashing U.S. Military Drone Dominance” initiative as a pivotal catalyst. The program is expected to channel additional funding into unmanned systems procurement and R&D, with emphasis on drones designed for frontline modularity and interoperability.

AeroVironment’s (AVAV) design philosophy, favoring “plug-and-play” components and common handheld ground systems, positions it well to capture that demand.

BlueHalo acquisition expands exposure

AeroVironment’s (AVAV) recent acquisition of BlueHalo broadens its reach into high-growth areas such as counter-drone solutions and advanced defense software.

The fiscal 2025 defense budget includes $2.4 billion earmarked for unmanned systems and $17.2 billion across science and technology accounts spanning AI, 5G, autonomy, microelectronics, space and quantum science. These categories have grown at mid-single digits annually since 2010, offering long-term opportunities for contractors like AeroVironment, according to BofA.

Competition rising, but incumbent strengths hold

While the analysts expect more competition as the DoW expands its Defense Innovation Unit’s “Blue List” to include new commercial drone providers, they argue AeroVironment (AVAV) retains a competitive edge. The success of its Switchblade loitering munition in Ukraine, coupled with proven production capacity and established relationships, provides insulation against emerging rivals.

Valuation and forecasts

BofA’s coverage initiation included long-term earnings growth expectations. Adjusted EPS is projected to rise from $2.99 in FY24 to $5.15 in FY28, with growth accelerating in the latter years. However, lofty valuation multiples reflect high investor expectations: the stock currently trades at 91x 2024 earnings, compressing to 53 times by 2028.

Despite elevated multiples, BofA believes AeroVironment’s (AVAV) strategic positioning, expanding technology suite, and strong policy tailwinds justify upside from current levels. The bank set a price objective of $300 on AeroVironment (AVAV) shares.

data.title: Natura rallies after striking a deal to sell the Avon business in Europe, Africa, and Asia
data.description:
Natura Cosmeticos SA (OTCPK:NTCOY) rallied on Thursday after the Brazilian beauty company announced the sale of its Avon International business that operates in Europe, Africa, and Asia to Regent. 

The move is expected to focus on its core brand and products. For investors, removing the financial drag of Avon and its debt is anticipated to allow the company more flexibility to make payments to shareholders in the future. CEO João Paulo Ferreira noted that Natura (OTCPK:NTCOY) wants to return to being a “high dividend yield” company.

Avon Russia is not included in this agreement and remains classified as "held for sale." The divestiture of Avon International does not affect the company's operations in Latin America, which remain at the core of Natura's (OTCPK:NTCOY) strategic priorities. The intellectual property rights and the management of the Avon brand in Latin America remain under Natura's (OTCPK:NTCOY) control.

"This agreement represents the final steps of the simplification journey initiated three years ago focused on the growth of our operations in Latin America. Avon International has already been implementing a robust transformation agenda, and we believe this journey will be strengthened, resuming sustainable growth of this iconic brand that has transformed the global beauty market over the past 139 years," stated Fábio Barbosa, Chairman of Natura's (OTCPK:NTCOY) Board of Directors.

On September 15, the company announced the sale of Avon's assets in Guatemala, Nicaragua, Panama, Honduras, El Salvador, and the Dominican Republic (Avon CARD) to Grupo PDC.

Shares of Natura (OTCPK:NTCOY) rallied 13% in São Paulo trading on Thursday.

data.title: Risk of a U.S. government shutdown rise as Democrats seek leverage - reports
data.description:
Leaders of the Democratic party are now embracing the possibility of a partial U.S. government shutdown on Oct. 1, as they seek to reverse Medicaid cuts that were part of President Donald Trump's signature $4T tax and spending cut legislation, according to media reports.

Just six months ago, Democrats ceded to Trump and congressional Republicans to avoid a shutdown, on the fear that the president was too popular to oppose. But now they're under pressure from their base to try to prevent a jump in Obamacare premiums and unwind hefty Medicaid cuts enacted in the so-called "One Big, Beautiful Bill."

On Tuesday, Democratic leaders warned that they wouldn't support a stop-gap spending bill to avoid a partial government shutdown because it doesn't address their concerns about soaring health insurance coverage costs for millions of Americans, the Associated Press reported. The proposal from Republicans would keep federal agencies funded through Nov. 21, giving legislators more time to hash out spending levels and policy for the fiscal year that starts on Oct. 1.

Meanwhile, Republicans contend that they're providing what Democrats have asked for, a clean funding bill without partisan policy riders. On Wednesday, the House of Representatives cleared the way for a vote later this week on the stopgap measure. If it passes the House, it will then go to the Senate, which will require a super-majority.

That means Republicans will need at least seven Democratic votes to get through the Senate, Bloomberg News reported.

In that case, Democrats, which are in the minority in both the House and the Senate, can exert significant leverage if minority leader Chuck Schumer can keep his members united. Democrats have issued a proposal that would extend government funding and reverse Medicaid cuts enacted in July, a move that could cost hundreds of billions of dollars.

However, Senate Majority Leader John Thune had earlier said that any counteroffer with major healthcare provisions tacked onto a short-term spending bill is "totally unserious." He said discussions about Obama subsidies can take place at a later date.

The lack of communication between Republican and Democratic leaders also increase the chances of a government shutdown at the beginning of the new fiscal year.

As has been the case in the past couple of decades, Congress has generally waited until the last minute to resolve such standoffs, so the posturing could go on for another week or more, Bloomberg News said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Monday.com pops as Wall Street optimistic after analyst day
data.description:
Shares of Israeli project management software firm monday.com (NASDAQ:MNDY) rose more than 6% on Thursday, as analysts were excited about what they heard at the company's investor day.

Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Thomas Blakey said monday.com's Elevate conference — and subsequent analyst day — highlighted its ability to cross-sell and boost adoption of new artificial intelligence solutions.

“Management addressed SEO highlighting Google Search was ~10% of new ARR, and they have alternative digital channels (i.e., YouTube) as well as other [go-to-market] changes that could offset pressures here tied to AI and Google Search, albeit these changes could elongate sales cycles,” Blakey wrote in a note to clients. “Financial updates included new metrics regarding ARR from all segments, not just CRM (last quarter disclosing $100m ARR, or HSD% of total by our calculation). The new disclosures were not far off from our modeling and denote near 20% growth in core Work Mgt. ARR in 2Q25, by our estimation.”

Blakey, who lowered his price target to $257 from $286, but kept his Overweight rating on the stock, said he is still positive on monday.com's ability to grow its core work management business at a “solid” double-digit percentage. In addition, new products should boost revenue growth to 20% or more, while the success in monetizing AI is not yet “fully baked into this outlook.”

Piper Sandler analyst Brent Bracelin was also positive after the analyst day. And while he lowered his price target to $275 from $300, he cited several positives from the event: the formal 2027 revenue target of $1.8B; strong work in AI and agentic work; tweaking its customer relationship management product to use more AI; and “solid progress” on its hybrid pricing model.

“The risk-reward on MNDY appears compelling after the 45% sell-off from the 52- week highs has pressured the NTM EV/S and EV/FCF multiples near the 4-year troughs at 6.5x and 23x, respectively,” Bracelin wrote in a note to clients.

data.title: U.S. stocks uplifted by Intel-Nvidia deal, a day after Fed's 25 bp rate cut
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were higher on Thursday, with the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) and the S&P 500 (SP500) rallying, a day after the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.7% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.4%.

Markets were helped by strong premarket action from tech firms Intel (INTC) +28.1% and Nvidia (NVDA) +3.5% after the firms announced a blockbuster deal on Thursday to co-develop PC and data center chips. Simultaneously, Nvidia said it would take a $5B stake in Intel.

Also, the FOMC on Wednesday announced its decision, trimming its policy rate by 25 basis points to 4.00%-4.25% on Wednesday. Miran dissented, arguing for a 50-basis-point cut.

“The Federal Reserve is cutting interest rates during a time when stocks are at record highs and the economy is still growing, which is a very unique backdrop, as Fed cuts are typically associated with a problematic economy. This dynamic is bullish for stocks,” said Robert Schein, CIO at Blanke Schein Wealth Management.

“Now that rates have been moving lower and the Federal Reserve is looking to cut rates a few more times, we are even more bullish on big tech and financials stocks. Big tech stocks tend to outperform during lower interest rate environments, and financials may see a boost from additional M&A and mortgage activity that may come about from lower rates,” Schein added.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.12%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.58%.

On the economic side, initial jobless claims dropped more than expected in the past week. Also, the Philly Fed Manufacturing Index jumped in September, blowing past consensus.

The August U.S. Leading Economic Index fell more than expected in August, -0.5% MoM to 98.4.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: Advanced Drainage System in talks to buy Norma's $1B water unit - report
data.description:
Advanced Drainage System (NYSE:WMS) is in advanced discussions to purchase the water management business of Germany's Norma Group SE (OTCPK:NOEJF) in a deal valued at about €850 million ($1 billion).

Advanced Drainage (NYSE:WMS) made a binding offer last week and a deal may be reached in the coming days, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

No final decision has been made, and other offers could come forward, according to the report. 

Advanced Drainage didn't respond to a Bloomberg request for comment, and Norma had no immediate comment. 

Shares of Advanced Drainage rose 1.5% on Thursday.

data.title: SEC clears Cboe, Nasdaq, NYSE to fast-track crypto ETP listings
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday cleared Cboe Global Markets (BATS:CBOE), Nasdaq (NASDAQ:NDAQ) and Intercontinental Exchange's (NYSE:ICE) New York Stock Exchange to list and trade commodity-based trust shares under fast-track standards, including crypto exchange-traded products.

The measure applies so long as the exchanges "meet the requirements of the approved generic listing standards," the SEC said in a statement.

“By approving these generic listing standards, we are ensuring that our capital markets remain the best place in the world to engage in the cutting-edge innovation of digital assets," said SEC Chairman Paul Atkins. This approval helps to maximize investor choice and foster innovation by streamlining the listing process and reducing barriers to access digital asset products within America’s trusted capital markets."

The move comes after regulators said that registered exchanges can in fact facilitate the trading of certain spot crypto asset products, including margin, leverage or financed so-called retail commodity transactions. The SEC also unveiled its agenda covering proposed rules tied to the offer and sale of crypto assets to help clarify the regulatory framework.

In addition to the approval of the generic listing standards, the SEC OK'd the listing and trading of the Grayscale Digital Large Cap Fund (OTC:GDLC), as well as p.m.-settled options on the Cboe Bitcoin U.S. ETF Index and the Mini-Cboe Bitcoin U.S. ETF Index with third Friday expirations, nonstandard expirations and quarterly index expirations.

GDLC holds spot digital assets based on the CoinDesk 5 Index. The SEC's decision allows the multi-token fund to be converted into an ETF. Based on a fact sheet, GDLC holdings were mostly bitcoin (BTC-USD) and ether (ETH-USD), with respective weights of 72% and 17%.

data.title: Krispy Kreme rises again on FBI director's surprise endorsement
data.description:
After spiking Wednesday in reaction to remarks from FBI Director Kash Patel, Krispy Kreme (NASDAQ:DNUT) is again on the move, rallying another 10% Thursday, albeit on light volume.

During an appearance before the House Judiciary Committee on Wednesday in which the head of the FBI was asked about buying stocks while holding a government position, Patel answered that he simply saw a good investment opportunity in Krispy Kreme (NASDAQ:DNUT) and ON Semiconductor (ON).

“I just follow certain industries,” he said.

Patel’s seemingly benign remark launched DNUT up by 11% within minutes before surrendering a bulk of its gains later in the day, closing Wednesday’s trading session up with a gain of less than 1%.

Patel’s stake in DNUT was apparently made months ago when he bought between $15,000 and $50,000 worth of shares in the company. He also purchased between $50,000 and $100,000 worth of ON Semiconductor (ON).

The spike in DNUT was most likely fueled by its meme-like characteristics which resulted in an 83% rally over a three-day period in July. Meme stocks tend to move dramatically with little to no fundamental catalyst.
